title: impl: fail closed on invalid bead dependency graph for phase-labeled work

- A mechanical check fails when an open review bead lacks at least one blocking dependency on earlier phase work for the same story

title: Refactor Ralph main iteration control flow into lib.sh helpers
description:
scripts/ralph/ralph.sh has grown to ~600 lines. The helper functions in the file are still small, but the top-level per-iteration control flow now owns bead selection, agent invocation, promise routing, retry handling, gate reruns, confidence routing, and auto-land. Refactor the branch-heavy iteration body into named helpers in scripts/ralph/lib.sh so future changes are less brittle without changing behavior.

Acceptance criteria:
- Extract the main branch-heavy per-iteration control flow from scripts/ralph/ralph.sh into named helpers in scripts/ralph/lib.sh. At minimum cover signal routing and post-BEAD_DONE gate/confidence/landing handling.
- scripts/ralph/ralph.sh becomes materially smaller and reads as orchestration rather than implementation detail.
- Existing behavior remains unchanged: COMPLETE routing, BLOCKED/REWORK handling, retry escalation, gate rerun, confidence routing, landing, and logging all still behave the same.
- tests/hooks/ralph.bats is updated to exercise the new helpers directly where appropriate instead of depending only on source-line extraction from ralph.sh.
- Verification gate passes.
close_reason: Extracted Ralph iteration control flow into lib.sh helpers and updated tests to cover the helpers directly.

title: review: cross-bead review of upstream-harness-improvements back-port (Tier 1-3)
description:
Read-only review covering the cumulative effect of beads agent-template-65s, agent-template-nvd, agent-template-dvd, agent-template-a8g, agent-template-slc, agent-template-6eo, agent-template-t81, agent-template-d92, agent-template-vh5, agent-template-ebh, agent-template-7zb. Per the review-bead contract: only docs/reviews/<this-bead-id>.md may be modified; cite docs/skills/review-rubric.md clauses on every finding; use the validator-enforced P[123].<name> shape.

Set .current-bead-type=review at iter start.

Specific focus areas the back-port doc names as risk surfaces:

(1) Cross-bead variable hygiene. Stale-HEAD detection (agent-template-nvd) introduces _RALPH_HEAD_AT_ITER_START. Bead-meta surfacing (agent-template-d92) computes _RALPH_COMPLETED_SUMMARY gated on stale-HEAD. Confirm the gating order is correct and the cleanup unset list covers every new global from both beads. _ralph_load_bead_meta (agent-template-d92) must run before _ralph_surface_stale_governance (agent-template-ebh).

(2) Pattern-citation gate (agent-template-vh5) against live CLAUDE.md. Verify every existing ## Discovered Patterns entry was retrofitted with a citation in the same bead, not deferred. List entries that are uncited.

(3) retry_count retirement (agent-template-7zb). Grep for orphan references in lib.sh, ralph.sh, tests, comments, prompt.md, archive.txt. Stray references after retirement are P2.weak-test (the test thinks an axis still exists when it doesn't) or worse.

(4) Verification gate clause count (agent-template-t81's gate_clause_count.bats). Confirm CLAUDE.md's gate string is unchanged in clause count over this back-port — no new top-level && clauses crept in (cross-cutting invariant #2).

(5) budgets.bats calibration (agent-template-6eo). Caps must be calibrated to the *current* harness sizes, not the inflated post-back-port sizes. If the back-port itself pushed a function past the pinned cap, the bead should have raised the cap with documented justification per the harness-pare workflow, not silently shipped under it.

(6) Cross-cutting invariant compliance per cumulative diff. Pause-rate ceiling (agent-template-t81's pause_rate_budget): does the cumulative effect of dropping retry_count + adding loop_saturation + governance-bead >7d → LOW + integration-block predicate stay at or below the prior baseline against the recorded fixture? Axis count (agent-template-t81's compute_confidence_arity): preserved at 4? Surface budget (agent-template-6eo): every harness file under cap?

(7) Fail-modes register integrity (agent-template-a8g's register_symbol_refs_check). Confirm the new check is wired AFTER each register's existing file-refs-check (composability, not replacement). Confirm every existing register row's <path>::<symbol> citation resolves under the new check; if any rows fail, list them as P2.register-gap.

Findings template: each P1/P2/P3 finding cites the rubric clause, names the bead it's against, identifies the specific artifact (file:line), and proposes a concrete fix (or follow-up bead title).
acceptance_criteria: docs/reviews/<this-bead-id>.md exists. Cites docs/skills/review-rubric.md. Every finding cites a P[123].<name> clause from the rubric (validated by review_artifact_clauses_check). Each of the seven focus areas above gets at least a 'no findings' or 'findings: …' line so the reviewer is on record for each. No source files outside docs/reviews/ are modified. Test: bats tests/hooks/ still passes.

title: Add pre-push hook that re-runs the verification gate
description:
Close the gate-bypass gap (Goodhart / P1.gate-bypass).

**Problem:** `scripts/ralph/ralph.sh` (lines 313-319) parses `<gate-result>PASS</gate-result>` from the agent's own output. `GATE_RESULT` is logged but not wired into `AUTO_LAND`, so the agent can emit a PASS tag without running the gate. `project-kickoff-prompt.md` line 197 acknowledges the hole and even names the fix but does not ship it.

**Fix:** Add a `.git/hooks/pre-push` hook (installed by `scripts/hooks/install.sh`) that:
1. Extracts the verification gate command from `CLAUDE.md` (convention: fenced code block under `## Verification Gate`).
2. Runs it via `bash -c` and captures the real exit code.
3. Fails the push if the observed result diverges from the self-reported `<gate-result>` tag.

Reference sketch in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 1 (lines 21-50).

**Follow-ups in same bead:**
- Update decision register's "Verification truth" row: `ritual-bounded` → `bounded`.
- Remove the prose caveat at `project-kickoff-prompt.md` line 197 (instance of the repo's own "promote, don't repeat" rule).
- Document the split in `CLAUDE.md`.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-push hook extracts gate command from CLAUDE.md, re-runs it, fails if exit code diverges from agent's self-reported <gate-result> tag. Installer block added to scripts/hooks/install.sh. Decision register's 'Verification truth' row promoted from ritual-bounded to bounded. Prose caveat at project-kickoff-prompt.md line 197 removed once hook lands.

title: ralph.sh runs verification gate directly; delete agent <gate-result> self-report
description: Today the agent runs the gate and emits <gate-result>PASS|FAIL</gate-result>; ralph.sh greps the tag and writes .last-gate-result from the self-report. The pre-push hook is the only real check. title: ralph: log real bead id on BEAD_DONE in confidence.log
description:
The two BEAD_DONE echo lines in scripts/ralph/ralph.sh use ${_RALPH_ACTIVE_BEAD:-unknown}, but _RALPH_ACTIVE_BEAD is empty whenever the iter started with no in-progress bead (the agent picks up a fresh bead via _ralph_bead_ready during the iter). Result: confidence.log shows bead=unknown for every successful BEAD_DONE iter that started clean. archive_schema_check filters bead=unknown out before requiring archive entries, so it silently passes.

Fix: change both echoes (ralph.sh:407 and ralph.sh:417) to ${_RALPH_BEAD_ID:-unknown}. _RALPH_BEAD_ID is set at the top of the iter from either the resumed _RALPH_ACTIVE_BEAD or _ralph_bead_ready, so it always names the bead the agent will work on.

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-27 Change 4.
acceptance_criteria: Both BEAD_DONE log lines reference _RALPH_BEAD_ID, not _RALPH_ACTIVE_BEAD. New bats test in tests/hooks/ralph.bats simulates an iter that started without an in-progress bead but picked up a ready bead, and asserts the resulting log line contains the picked-up id (not 'unknown'). archive_schema_check now actually fires on real ids in confidence.log.

title: tests/hooks/budgets.bats — per-file and per-function harness size caps
description:
Cross-cutting invariant #4: harness implementation surface is bounded by line/function caps that fail at commit, triggering a harness-pare bead. Today's largest function is rubric_edit_check (~46 lines); largest file is parsers.bats at 736 (test file) and parsers.sh at 472 (impl). Ship caps calibrated to current healthy state plus modest headroom so the next ADD triggers the review, not the current state.

New bats file under tests/hooks/ is absorbed by the existing 'bats tests/hooks/' gate clause (no gate-string growth, honors invariant #2). Per-file line caps for ralph.sh, lib.sh, parsers.sh, install.sh. Per-function size cap via awk that walks function definitions and computes max block length.

Header comment documents that cap-raises require a harness-pare bead with documented justification (per cross-cutting invariant #4 in docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md).

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-29 (harness surface-area budgets entry).
acceptance_criteria: tests/hooks/budgets.bats exists. Asserts per-file line caps (calibrated to current sizes + modest headroom; specific values pinned in test). Asserts per-function size cap via awk that walks function definitions and computes the max block length across ralph.sh / lib.sh / parsers.sh / install.sh. Test passes against current tree. title: Final review: verify all initializer-audit changes against recommendations
description:
Gate bead for the initializer-audit set. Runs after impl beads 1–4 close.

**Procedure:**
1. Re-read `initializer-recommendations.md` start-to-finish.
2. For each of sections 1–4, verify the shipped code against the recommendation:
   - Does the mechanism exist where the recommendation said it should?
   - Does it do what the recommendation described (not just compile)?
   - Are the downstream promotions applied (decision-register row changes, prose-caveat deletion)?
3. Run `scripts/hooks/install.sh` against a clean worktree and confirm hooks install without error.
4. Run the new `tests/hooks/` suite and confirm green.
5. Simulate a gate-bypass attempt: emit `<gate-result>PASS</gate-result>` with a gate command that exits non-zero, and confirm the pre-push hook blocks it.
6. Simulate an unedited-rubric commit (once a bead exists) and confirm the pre-commit hook blocks it.

**If any check fails:** create a new impl bead that specifies the missing work, mark it `blocks` this review bead, and leave this bead open. Do not close until every downstream bead is closed and re-verified.

**Explicitly out of scope:** adding new audit findings. This bead verifies the four recommendations on file, not the whole codebase.
acceptance_criteria: Every fix in initializer-recommendations.md verified against the shipped implementation: (1) pre-push hook re-runs gate and blocks divergent self-reports; 'Verification truth' is now 'bounded'; prose caveat removed. (2) tests/hooks/ bats suite runs green and covers the listed edge cases; CONTRIBUTING.md orders parser tests before end-to-end. (3) rubric-edit hook blocks unedited starter; 'Review verdict' now 'bounded'. (4) CONTRIBUTING.md cleanup block carries inline comments. If any fix falls short, open new impl beads (blocking this one) that specify the missing work; do not close this review bead until all downstream beads are closed and verified.

title: Tighten rubric-edit guard: check for project-specificity, not just disclaimer phrase
description:
Discovered by review agent-template-6ij as P2.weak-test.

The rubric-edit guard in scripts/hooks/install.sh:163 fires only when 'This file is a starter rubric' literal phrase matches via grep -qF. A future edit that simply deletes that one sentence — without renaming the header, without adding any project-specific clause — passes the guard. The hook's BLOCKED message accurately describes the Phase 1 contract (replace the disclaimer + add at least one project-specific clause) but only the disclaimer half is enforced.

Suggested fix: in addition to the disclaimer check, assert (a) the rubric header line does not equal the shipped starter header verbatim AND (b) the rubric contains at least one clause that is not in a checked-in 'starter clauses' allowlist. The allowlist could live in scripts/hooks/install.sh as a named array and ship as the authoritative starter set.

Acceptance:
- Guard rejects a stub rubric with no clauses (currently passes).
- Guard rejects a rubric that copies the starter clauses verbatim with no additions.
- Guard accepts the current docs/skills/review-rubric.md (which has a project-named header and a project-specific P1.hook-bypass clause).
- New test in tests/hooks/ exercises all three cases.

title: Run secrets and dependency checks fail-closed in CI
description: Review finding: gitleaks and dep-hallucinator are active local pre-commit checks, but they warn and continue when missing and are not run by CI. For a load-bearing backend, local bootstrap can stay fail-warn, but CI should install and run equivalent checks fail-closed so missing local tooling cannot be the only enforcement layer.

Preserve reasonable Phase 1 local bootstrap behavior if desired. Add documentation describing local fail-warn vs CI fail-closed semantics. Add tests or workflow guards where practical. title: lib.sh: retire retry_count axis; add loop_saturation axis (axis-budget one-in-one-out)
description:
Two coupled changes that honor cross-cutting invariant #3 (axis budget one-in-one-out): retire one axis, add one axis, net count unchanged.

RETIRE retry_count: a silent-fail iter (no exit signal) bumps _RALPH_FAIL_COUNT; the next successful iter snapshots that count BEFORE the BEAD_DONE branch resets it, then passes it as retry_count to compute_confidence which downgrades to MEDIUM. Once gate=PASS, the prior struggle is amortized — there is no correctness signal in retry_count. Drop the 5th positional parameter from compute_confidence; remove the [[ retry_count -gt 0 ]] downgrade line; remove _RALPH_FAIL_COUNT_AT_ITER_START snapshot from ralph.sh; drop it from compute_confidence call site; drop from cleanup unset list. Flip the retry_count>0→MEDIUM test in tests/hooks/ralph.bats into a pin that the function ignores any 5th legacy arg.

ADD loop_saturation: a runtime detector for the recursive scope-tightening pattern. compute_confidence accepts a 5th axis 'recent_followup_ratio' — fraction of last N closed beads with 'Phase % follow-up:' titles. Downgrade once when ratio > 0.6 over the last 5 closed beads AND no 'Phase N impl:' or 'integration-' bead has closed in that window. ralph.sh computes the ratio at iter end via 'bd list --status=closed --json | jq' over the last 5 and echoes followup_ratio=N/5 into confidence.log.

Update tests/hooks/compute_confidence_arity.bats from bead F2 if its pinned axis count needs adjustment (the count is preserved at 4, so the test should still pass).

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-29 (runaway-loop entry) Changes 2 (count-citation) and 5 (loop_saturation axis); 2026-04-27 Change 1 (retry_count drop).
acceptance_criteria: compute_confidence: 5th positional param is recent_followup_ratio (not retry_count). Downgrades when ratio > 0.6 AND no Phase N impl: or integration- bead in the window of 5 closed beads. retry_count downgrade line removed. ralph.sh: _RALPH_FAIL_COUNT_AT_ITER_START removed; new ratio computed and passed; LOOP_SATURATION echo line added on downgrade path; followup_ratio=N/5 in confidence.log on BEAD_DONE. tests/hooks/ralph.bats: bracket tests around 0.6 cut (3/5 stays prevailing, 4/5 downgrades to MEDIUM); legacy retry_count>0 test flipped to assert HIGH with arbitrary 5th legacy arg shape; assertion that LOOP_SATURATION log line fires. tests/hooks/compute_confidence_arity.bats from F2 still passes.

title: Fix verification gate: trailing '|| true' masks failures in the whole chain
description:
The verification gate in CLAUDE.md reads:

  bash -n scripts/ralph/ralph.sh && bash -n scripts/hooks/install.sh && bash -n scripts/ralph/prompt.md 2>/dev/null || true

The trailing '|| true' was intended to suppress the expected failure of 'bash -n' on prompt.md (it's markdown, not bash). But due to shell precedence, '|| true' binds to the whole '&&' chain, so ANY failure in the chain (including real syntax errors in ralph.sh or install.sh) returns exit 0. The gate is currently a structural no-op.

Demonstrated:
  $ echo 'foo(' > /tmp/broken.sh
  $ bash -n /tmp/broken.sh && echo ok || true ; echo $?
  # → exit 0 despite parse failure

This also neutralizes the pre-push hook added by agent-template-4mw — the hook re-runs whatever the gate is, and if the gate always returns 0, the hook always passes.

Fix options:
  (a) Drop the prompt.md parse check entirely (markdown should never be parse-checked as bash).
  (b) Isolate the last clause: '... && { bash -n scripts/ralph/prompt.md 2>/dev/null || true; }'
  (c) Replace prompt.md check with a real markdown lint or drop it.

Acceptance: the gate in CLAUDE.md returns non-zero when any in-chain command fails.

title: Make Ralph distinguish bd failures from no ready work
description: Review finding: scripts/hooks/parsers.sh correctly fail-closes when bd list --json errors, but ralph.sh helper functions return empty on bd or jq failures. That can make the loop behave as if no bead is active or ready during tracker/daemon/JSON problems, rather than surfacing an operational failure.
acceptance_criteria: Update ralph.sh bead state helpers or their call sites to distinguish an empty ready/in-progress list from bd/jq extraction failure. On tracker failure, emit a clear error or BLOCKED-style route rather than silently treating it as no work. Add bats coverage for bd command failure, non-parseable JSON, empty-list success, and normal success. Verification gate passes.
close_reason: Ralph now verifies unfinished beads before honoring COMPLETE, and tests cover open/in_progress/bd-failure paths.

title: Broaden gate.bats trailing-soft-fail check beyond '|| true'
description:
Discovered by review agent-template-6ij as P2.weak-test.

tests/hooks/gate.bats test 2 asserts the gate command does not end with the regex '[[:space:]]*\|\|[[:space:]]*true[[:space:]]*$'. The bug class documented in docs/failure-modes.md row 14 is broader: any soft-fail escape via shell precedence (|| true, || :, || 0, || exit 0, etc.) silently masks all earlier && clauses. Reproduced: replacing the gate with '... && bats tests/hooks/ || :' passes test 2 (the colon noop is functionally identical to || true but isn't matched).

The bug class is rescued by tests 6/7 (corruption tests detect that ralph.sh / parsers.sh syntax errors no longer fail the chain), but the named test 2 has a coverage gap that future agents reading it as the canonical defense will not notice.

Suggested fix: broaden test 2's regex to cover all bash builtins/operators that always-succeed: 'true', ':', '0', 'exit 0'. Or replace with a behavioral check: build a tiny temp gate '(false) <suffix>' and assert it exits non-zero.

Acceptance:
- gate.bats test 2 fails for all of: '|| true', '|| :', '|| 0', '|| exit 0' suffixes.
- gate.bats test 2 passes for the current gate.
- docs/failure-modes.md row 14 updated to point at the broadened check (or a renamed check).

Verified failure scenario reproduced in docs/reviews/agent-template-6ij.md.

title: hooks: register_symbol_refs_check resolves <path>::<symbol> citations in registers
description:
scripts/hooks/parsers.sh fm_file_refs_check / dec_file_refs_check validate path-shaped tokens exist on disk but treat the ::<symbol> suffix as opaque. A pare-down that renames or deletes a register-cited test silently breaks the row's coverage claim while file-existence still holds. CLAUDE.md's discipline 'Register Check-cell citations bind the named symbol's existence-by-name' was being held by manual cross-referencing.

Add register_symbol_refs_check to parsers.sh that walks <path>::<symbol> tokens in both registers and asserts the symbol is defined as one of: 'def <symbol>(', 'async def <symbol>(', 'class <symbol>(' / 'class <symbol>:', or module-level '<symbol> = …' / '<symbol>: <type> = …'. Use grep with word boundaries (not literal substring) to avoid impostor matches (test_boom vs test_boom_extended).

Skip refs whose file is missing (delegated to existing file-refs-check) and gitignored files (no checked-in source to grep). On dangling refs, list every offender so a single commit can fix in batch.

Wire into install.sh after fm_file_refs_check and dec_file_refs_check.

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-29 (register-symbol resolution entry).
acceptance_criteria: register_symbol_refs_check accepts: def, async def, class with bases, class without bases, module-level assignment, annotated module-level assignment. Rejects: cited test deleted, cited helper renamed, substring impostor (test_boom vs test_boom_extended). Skips: missing file, gitignored file. install.sh wires the check after each register's file-refs-check. tests/hooks/parsers.bats covers accept/reject/skip cases plus smoke tests against the live registers. Lists every offender on failure (not just the first). Works for .py/.sh/.bats/.md files (grep-based, not AST).

title: compound: harness-prune-compound — re-validate registers and patterns post-prune
description:
Final pass over the pruned harness. With register_symbol_refs_check and pattern_citation_check both retired, the structural backstops on register row freshness and pattern citations are gone — this bead re-validates them by hand and writes any surviving discoveries forward as durable patterns.

Scope (.current-bead-scope):
  CLAUDE.md
  docs/failure-modes.md
  docs/decision-register.md
  docs/skills/

Definition of done:
- Walk every row in docs/failure-modes.md. For each path::symbol or path:test reference, manually verify the symbol/test still exists in the cited file. Fix any dangling reference or delete the row if the bug class it documented has been retired in the prune chain.
- Walk every row in docs/decision-register.md. Same check. Verify each Bounding-mechanism file path still exists.
- Walk every '### ' entry under CLAUDE.md '## Discovered Patterns'. For each, verify the model: tag is present and the binding artifact citation (path::symbol, tests/... path, or registers reference) still resolves. Retire patterns whose binding artifact was deleted in this chain.
- Audit the chain's commits: confirm no commit re-introduced a retired check (e.g., the agent reflexively adding back a soft-fail-escape regex while editing). If found, file a follow-up bead.
- Append a single-paragraph entry to scripts/ralph/archive.txt summarizing what the chain pruned (file count, line count, retired hook list) so a future audit reading the log can find this transition cleanly.
- Verification gate green.
- Commit message: 'compound: [<bead-id>] - validate registers and patterns post-harness-prune'

This is the bitter-lesson backstop: with structural validators retired, the human review (this bead) is the last line of defense. After this bead lands, the harness is in its lean steady-state.

Depends on: agent-template-2zj.

title: Make Ralph auto-land by committing only via the agent and pushing itself
description:
Today ralph.sh's auto-land path only means 'do not pause' after a green BEAD_DONE. It does not run the session-completion ritual from AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md: no clean-tree check, no git pull --rebase, no bd sync, no push, and no final origin parity check. This leaves beads stranded locally and makes the human finish the landing sequence manually.

Scope:
- scripts/ralph/ralph.sh
- scripts/ralph/lib.sh
- scripts/ralph/prompt.md
- tests/hooks/ralph.bats
- docs/decision-register.md
- docs/failure-modes.md
- README.md
- CLAUDE.md

Acceptance criteria:
- Ralph fail-closes BEAD_DONE if the worktree is dirty before the landing ritual, rather than pretending auto-land happened.
- Ralph auto-land path runs git pull --rebase, bd sync, commits the resulting beads-state update when needed, pushes, and verifies main is up to date with origin.
- A landing failure blocks further auto-land progress and is logged with a machine-readable reason.
- The prompt contract tells the agent that BEAD_DONE requires the implementation commit to already exist; Ralph owns only the post-bead landing ritual.
- Bats coverage exercises the clean-tree gate and the landing helper success/failure cases.
- Verification gate passes.
close_reason: Ralph now performs the post-bead landing ritual and fails closed on dirty-tree or push errors.

title: Review artifact validator: enforce clause-name membership against docs/skills/review-rubric.md
description:
Discovered by review agent-template-6ij as P2.weak-test.

The review artifact validator in scripts/hooks/install.sh:397 requires at least one match of the regex 'P[123]\.[a-z][a-z-]*' in a review artifact. It does not check membership against the actual clauses defined in docs/skills/review-rubric.md. Reproduced: a review artifact citing 'P1.totally-made-up-clause' (no such clause in the rubric) passes the validator with exit 0.

The decision-register row 'Review verdict' (docs/decision-register.md:11) claims 'bounded' by 'each finding cites a clause' — but the binding is to clause-shaped strings, not to clauses that exist. This is a Goodhart on the validator itself.

Suggested fix: extract the canonical clause list from docs/skills/review-rubric.md (every '**P[123]\.<name>**' bullet under '## Severity definitions') and assert each cited clause in the review artifact is in that list. Reject any cited clause not in the rubric with a BLOCKED message naming the offending clause. New parser function in scripts/hooks/parsers.sh + new tests in tests/hooks/.

Acceptance:
- Validator rejects review artifact citing P1.invented-clause if no matching clause exists in the rubric.
- Validator accepts review artifact citing only clauses that exist in the rubric.
- Adding a new clause to docs/skills/review-rubric.md immediately makes that clause valid (no separate registration step).
- New tests/hooks/ tests for the membership check.

Verified failure scenario reproduced in docs/reviews/agent-template-6ij.md.

title: Derive <confidence> from signals in lib.sh; remove agent self-grade
description: Today the agent self-grades HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW in a <confidence> tag and ralph.sh trusts it. Change: new lib.sh function compute_confidence(gate_result, diff_lines, touched_hooks, touched_claude_md, retry_count) returns HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW deterministically. ralph.sh calls it on BEAD_DONE with git-show-derived signals. Removes parse_confidence and parse_confidence_bead_done and the corresponding prompt.md emission requirement. Acceptance: (1) compute_confidence in lib.sh with bats coverage for gate-FAIL terminal LOW, each downgrade axis, and multi-downgrade combinations; (2) ralph.sh gathers signals from git show HEAD on BEAD_DONE and routes via compute_confidence; (3) prompt.md drops the <confidence> tag requirement; (4) decision-register Confidence row updated to name compute_confidence as the bounding mechanism.

title: ralph: surface bead type/title/description in banner; emit bead_type/title/completed in confidence.log
description:
Five tightly-coupled changes per docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-28 (the doc explicitly recommends landing all five as one bead due to shared variables and sanitization).

(1) Add _ralph_load_bead_meta to ralph.sh: single bd show --json call that populates _RALPH_BEAD_TYPE / _RALPH_BEAD_TITLE / _RALPH_BEAD_DESCRIPTION. jq path uses '(if type == "array" then .[0] else . end).<field>' to handle bd <0.49 (object) and bd >=0.49 (array). Logical type derived from title via [[ "$_RALPH_BEAD_TITLE" =~ (^|[[:space:]])(impl|review|pare|pare-down|compound|research)[[:space:]]*: ]]; falls back to .issue_type.

(2) Banner replaces 'Resuming: id — title' / 'Current bead: id — title' with '[type] — title' plus separate 'Description: <desc>' line. Same hydrated globals.

(3) All five confidence.log echo points (BLOCKED, REWORK, ESCALATION, BEAD_DONE-with-confidence, BEAD_DONE-without-confidence) gain bead_type=$_RALPH_BEAD_TYPE and title="sanitized". BEAD_DONE branches additionally gain completed="sanitized". Sanitizer _ralph_sanitize_log_field collapses whitespace to single spaces, replaces " with ', truncates to 160 chars with ... marker.

(4) _RALPH_COMPLETED_SUMMARY captures HEAD's commit subject via 'git log -1 --pretty=%s', gated on stale-HEAD detection (uses helper from bead B): if stale, replace with '(no new commit — stale HEAD)'.

(5) Iteration footer prints '<id> [<type>] — <title>' and 'Completed: <subject>' before 'Iteration N complete.' separator, gated on _RALPH_COMPLETED_SUMMARY non-empty.

Also fix _ralph_bead_title's existing jq path '.title // empty' which silently produces empty on bd >=0.49 (array response).

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-28.
acceptance_criteria: _ralph_load_bead_meta populates the three globals; jq path handles array AND object bd output (bats test exercises both). Banner prints [type] — title plus Description line. All 5 log echo points carry bead_type= and title='...'; both BEAD_DONE branches carry completed='...'. _RALPH_COMPLETED_SUMMARY uses stale-HEAD helper (depends on bead B). Sanitizer collapses whitespace, replaces " with ', truncates to 160 with '...'. archive_schema_check parser regex 'bead=[^ ]+' still works (bead_type=<word> field does not collide). Footer shows bead context only when summary non-empty. Cleanup _ralph_cleanup unsets new globals. Bats coverage: array vs object jq, sanitizer edge cases, banner shape, log shape per branch.

title: ralph: scope touched_claude_md downgrade to edits outside ## Discovered Patterns
description:
ralph.sh uses inline 'grep -qx CLAUDE.md' to set _RALPH_TOUCHED_CLAUDE_MD. Compound beads in this template's quartet pattern routinely promote model-tagged entries to ## Discovered Patterns; those edits trigger a downgrade though they redefine no rule the gate runs (the section is by-design append-only output). Edits to ## Invariants or ## Verification Gate, by contrast, can shift the gate's own definition in the same diff being evaluated — that risk is what the axis is designed to catch.

Add claude_md_touched_outside_patterns helper to scripts/ralph/lib.sh that reads git show HEAD:CLAUDE.md and HEAD~1:CLAUDE.md, strips the ## Discovered Patterns section from both via awk (/^## Discovered Patterns[[:space:]]*$/{f=1;next} /^## /{f=0} !f), and compares the rest. Replace inline grep in ralph.sh with the function call.

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-27 Change 3.
acceptance_criteria: claude_md_touched_outside_patterns handles every edge case: pattern-only edit returns false (compound-bead happy path); ## Invariants edit returns true; ## Verification Gate clause edit returns true; no-CLAUDE-change returns false; file-added returns true; file-deleted returns true. 7+ bats tests in tests/hooks/ralph.bats using temp git repos. ralph.sh uses the new helper at the touched-claude-md detection point. Smoke test against the live repo asserts boolean shape only.

title: BLOCKED: agent-template-4al — ...
impl: harness-prune-hooks — strip install.sh to 5 essential checks
description:
Strip the pre-commit chain in scripts/hooks/install.sh down to the 5 hooks that defend currently-active bug classes. Drop the rest along with their parser functions and bats coverage in the same commit so the gate stays green.

Scope (.current-bead-scope):

title: ralph: surface stale governance beads above iter banner; force LOW at >7d staleness
description:
Stale governance beads (Observe / Audit / Decide / Triage / Review the loop) titled work that asks the loop's own meta-questions (e.g. 'Observe ralph loop for 20 impl beads; decide on research-phase split') get ranked by bd ready the same as everything else, so the loop preferentially picks module work over meta-questions with no obvious deliverable. The signal must reach operator visibility without depending on the agent self-prioritizing meta-work.

Add _ralph_surface_stale_governance to scripts/ralph/ralph.sh. After _ralph_load_bead_meta (from bead G) at iter start, greps 'bd list --status=open --json' for titles matching ^(Observe|Audit|Decide|Triage|Review the loop) with created_at >3 days. Prints them above the banner under '⚠ Stale governance:' with bead id, age in days, title.

Add governance_bead_max_age_days predicate to scripts/ralph/lib.sh: if the oldest stale governance bead is >7 days old, the predicate returns truthy; ralph.sh forces compute_confidence's gate path to LOW (the loop has been ignoring its own audit instruction for over a week).

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-29 (runaway-loop entry) Change 4.
acceptance_criteria: _ralph_surface_stale_governance prints stale governance beads above iter banner. governance_bead_max_age_days returns truthy at >7d, falsy at ≤7d (bracket tests around the cut: 7d stays, 8d forces). Predicate matches ^Observe… / ^Audit… etc; ignores ^Phase 3 impl: …. tests/hooks/ralph.bats covers: predicate accept/reject, 7d boundary, downgrade firing on >7d. ralph.sh forces LOW when predicate is truthy.

title: Add pre-commit hook that rejects unedited review-rubric starter
description:
Tightening (not a violation): `project-kickoff-prompt.md` line 105 states that the shipped starter alone does not satisfy the Phase 1 contract — an unedited starter means 'Review verdict' cannot legitimately be `bounded`. Every other Phase 1 contract that can be mechanically enforced is enforced; this is the only one where a one-line grep would promote `ritual-bounded` → `bounded` and reduce Phase-1-approval burden on the user.

**Fix:** Add the following block to the pre-commit hook generator in `scripts/hooks/install.sh`:

```bash
RUBRIC_FILE="$PROJECT_ROOT/docs/skills/review-rubric.md"
if [ -f "$RUBRIC_FILE" ]; then
  if grep -qF "This file is a starter rubric" "$RUBRIC_FILE"; then
    if [ -n "$IN_PROGRESS_BEAD" ]; then
      echo "BLOCKED: docs/skills/review-rubric.md still contains the starter disclaimer."
      # ... (full sketch in initializer-recommendations.md lines 140-157)
      exit 1
    fi
  fi
fi
```

Promote the "Review verdict" row in the decision register from `ritual-bounded` to `bounded` once this hook ships.

Reference sketch in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 3 (lines 127-159).
acceptance_criteria: Pre-commit hook block added to scripts/hooks/install.sh that rejects commits where docs/skills/review-rubric.md still contains the starter disclaimer once any bead exists (Phase 1 bootstrap exempted). 'Review verdict' row in decision register promoted from ritual-bounded to bounded once this hook ships.

title: Add automated parser tests for hook parsers (bats suite)
description:
Fill the Testing Pyramid gap: the awk parsers in the pre-commit hooks are the mechanism that every other register-integrity contract rests on, yet there are no automated tests.

**Problem:** `scripts/hooks/install.sh` (lines 256-525) and tag parsing in `scripts/ralph/ralph.sh` (lines 105-116, 205-222, 313-319) have real edge cases — escaped pipes, Unicode, exactly-5-column rows, trailing whitespace, multi-line continuation rows the hook is supposed to reject. `CONTRIBUTING.md` line 30 mandates an end-to-end ralph run but that does not exercise parser edge cases.

**Fix:**
1. Light refactor of `install.sh` to expose parser blocks as callable functions (currently inlined in the generated pre-commit script).
2. Add `tests/hooks/` with a bats or shunit2 suite. Cover at least:
   - fm register: rejects bad Status; accepts covered Status + existing check file.
   - decision register: rejects multi-line continuation rows.
   - CLAUDE.md model-tag validator: rejects unknown model tag; accepts valid tag.
   - Edge cases: escaped pipes, Unicode in cells, trailing whitespace.
3. Wire into `CONTRIBUTING.md` as the first verification step.

Reference sketch in `initializer-recommendations.md` section 2 (lines 68-123).
acceptance_criteria: tests/hooks/ directory contains bats (or shunit2) suite exercising failure-mode register integrity, decision register integrity, and CLAUDE.md model-tag validator parsers against known-good and known-bad fixtures (escaped pipes, Unicode cells, 5-column rows, trailing whitespace, multi-line continuations). scripts/hooks/install.sh refactored to expose parser blocks as callable functions so they can be tested outside of a live pre-commit context. title: ralph: detect BEAD_DONE without a new commit (stale-HEAD)
description:
Per-iter measurements (diff_lines / touched_hooks / touched_claude_md) read HEAD without verifying HEAD has moved since iter start. An iter that emits BEAD_DONE without committing grades the *prior* commit's diff, crediting that iter to the wrong work.

Add compute_head_unchanged_for_bead_done(pre_sha, post_sha) to scripts/ralph/lib.sh; capture pre-iter HEAD into _RALPH_HEAD_AT_ITER_START before the agent runs; on BEAD_DONE re-read HEAD, call the helper, and on unchanged=true force gate_result=FAIL plus emit stale_head=true into confidence.log.

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-27 (second entry, stale-HEAD).
acceptance_criteria: compute_head_unchanged_for_bead_done(pre,post) returns 0 (unchanged) / 1 (commit landed). ralph.sh captures pre-iter HEAD and re-reads after agent. On BEAD_DONE+unchanged: gate_result is forced to FAIL, confidence.log carries stale_head=true. Bats helper tests in tests/hooks/ralph.bats: returns 0 when HEAD moved in temp repo, 1 when stale. Integration test: stub agent emitting BEAD_DONE without committing is forced to FAIL. docs/failure-modes.md row binds stale_head=true to FAIL routing.

Definition of done:

    - Remove the 'negative-space proof' requirement entirely (the paragraph in §1 'The exhaustiveness contract' that requires a written argument 'the failure modes for this module are exactly the rows above, and here is why no other class of failure exists'). Replace with a softer framing: the failure-mode register lists rows that have come up; Phase 4 is when completeness is re-tested adversarially.
    - Add a 'What this template does not give you' section near the top (after 'Philosophy'). State plainly: structural enforcement is not a substitute for human review; a green gate is a license to propose a merge, not a guarantee of correctness; quality lives in the review and compound passes, not in the hooks.

The patterns it contained (bind-to-property-not-proxy, one-implementation-one-library, ritual→bounded promotion, gate-clause ordering, snapshot-volatile-state-once) were inherited template scar tissue; downstream projects rediscover what they need.

title: bug: _ralph_emit_log uses 'status' as local — read-only in zsh, aborts loop and leaks set -u
description:
zsh treats `status` as a special read-only parameter (alias of $?). `local status="$1"` at scripts/ralph/ralph.sh:129 fires `read-only variable: status` when ralph.sh is sourced from zsh, the function aborts before writing the confidence.log line, and the unwind skips `_ralph_cleanup` so `set -u` leaks back into the user's interactive shell (RPROMPT / VIRTUAL_ENV prompt-segment errors). Loop exits early despite open beads.

Reported error excerpt:
  _ralph_emit_log:1: read-only variable: status
  __vsc_update_prompt:6: RPROMPT: parameter not set
  prompt_virtualenv:1: VIRTUAL_ENV: parameter not set

Fix: rename the local to a non-special name (`emit_status`); add a regression test that sources ralph.sh and exercises _ralph_emit_log under zsh, asserting no read-only error and that `set -u` does not leak past _ralph_cleanup.
close_reason: Renamed scripts/ralph/ralph.sh:_ralph_emit_log local 'status' → 'emit_status' (zsh reserves status as a read-only special parameter). Added regression test in tests/hooks/ralph.bats that greps the ralph/hooks bash surface for any function-local declaration colliding with a zsh read-only special name (status, pipestatus, argv, funcstack, funcfiletrace, funcsourcetrace, signals, seconds, histcmd, history, lineno) and fails with the offending file:line. End-to-end zsh smoke confirmed: emit returns 0, log line is correct, set -u no longer leaks past _ralph_cleanup. All 149 bats tests green.

title: Observe ralph loop for 20 impl beads; decide on research-phase split
description:
Baseline observation bead. Before adopting Dexter Horthy's CRISPY-style phase split (research → design → implement as separate agent calls), we want evidence that the current one-shot agent call is struggling in ways a phase split would fix. Beads 5yh / d33 / 0ik closed the gate-result and confidence self-report gaps; the next structural change is the expensive one (more tokens, more artifacts, more bash orchestration), so it should be gated on measurement, not speculation.

## Preconditions

- confidence.log contains 20+ entries with bead_done=true and bead != unknown, dated 2026-04-23 or later (i.e., after compute_confidence / run_gate shipped). Earlier entries are under the old self-report design and the signals are not comparable. If the log has fewer than 20 qualifying entries, emit BLOCKED with reason 'insufficient data' and a count.

## Acceptance

1. docs/reviews/<bead-id>.md exists with sections: Methodology, Findings, Recommendation.
2. Findings section reports three ratios from the 20+ qualifying entries, segmented by bead type (impl vs other):
   a. Retry rate: fraction of beads that needed retry_count > 0 to finish. Segment by impl vs non-impl.
   b. Divergence rate: fraction of push-hook runs where observed != .last-gate-result (check git reflog / CI logs or filter confidence.log for entries where the next push blocked).
   c. Confidence distribution: HIGH vs MEDIUM vs LOW shares on BEAD_DONE, by bead type.
3. Recommendation section picks ONE of:
   - 'split Phase A research': if impl-bead retry rate > 20% OR impl-bead MEDIUM+LOW share > 30%, suggesting the agent is struggling to plan before coding. Cite the specific retries / MEDIUMs that a design-phase re-steer would plausibly have caught.
   - 'hold one-shot': if impl-bead retry rate <= 20% AND impl-bead MEDIUM+LOW share <= 30%, suggesting the current prompt is operating inside the instruction budget. The existing gate + register infrastructure is doing the work Dexter's design phase recreates.
   - 'different intervention': if the data shows a problem neither split would fix (e.g., divergence rate > 5% pointing at env drift, or LOW confidence concentrated on specific modules pointing at a skill-file gap). Name the intervention and file a follow-up bead.
4. If the recommendation is 'split Phase A research': file a follow-up bead (depends on this one) titled 'Implement Phase A research split for impl beads' with acceptance referencing the scripts/ralph/ artifacts to create (research_prompt.md, docs/reviews/<bead-id>-research.md, validator in parsers.sh).
5. No source files modified — this is a research bead, read-only analysis. Commit message: research: [bead-id] - <title>.

## Pointers for the next agent

- Runtime data: scripts/ralph/confidence.log (iteration log), .last-gate-result (gitignored, current only), git log (per-bead commits).
- Thresholds above are starting heuristics, not load-bearing rules. If the data cleanly resolves the question, cite the evidence and the threshold choice. If the data is ambiguous, say so and propose a measurement continuation bead rather than forcing a verdict.
- The philosophical frame (why we're measuring) is in recent archive.txt entries under beads 5yh / d33 / 0ik, and the full proposal for CRISPY is in the Horthy transcript discussion that preceded those beads. Do not re-derive from scratch.
- Do not run ralph.sh to generate synthetic data. The whole point is that we want to see real-workload behavior, not behavior on a test bead we contrived to hit a threshold.
close_reason: Question answered by running the loop in the downstream project; findings captured in docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md and beads filed for the back-port (agent-template-65s through -12x).

title: Grow verification gate to include parsers.sh syntax check and bats tests/hooks/
description:
Now that agent-template-8gi has removed the trailing '|| true' that silently masked every clause, the gate can safely grow. mhd's archive note explicitly deferred the bats append until 8gi shipped.

Append to the gate in CLAUDE.md:
  bash -n scripts/ralph/ralph.sh && bash -n scripts/hooks/install.sh && bash -n scripts/hooks/parsers.sh && bats tests/hooks/

Why parsers.sh: the pre-commit hook sources it at runtime (install.sh:77). A syntax error there breaks the whole register-integrity chain but is currently unguarded by the gate.

Why bats tests/hooks/: that's how parser regressions surface without chasing a loop failure (CONTRIBUTING.md already documents this as the manual step; bringing it into the gate makes it mechanical).

Update tests/hooks/gate.bats 'gate passes against the current repo' test to keep working under the expanded chain.

title: hooks: pattern_citation_check rejects ## Discovered Patterns entries without a binding artifact
description:
Cross-cutting bound on the pattern set: each ### entry under ## Discovered Patterns must cite a checked-in artifact (tests, register row, or symbol). Without this, the only structural rule on the pattern set is the 200-line CLAUDE.md cap — a count bound, not a content bound, and bytes can grow 4× while line count stays flat. The 'pare-down test: where is the bound?' discipline applied to the pattern set itself.

Add pattern_citation_check to scripts/hooks/parsers.sh. Walks every ### <title> block under ## Discovered Patterns and requires the body to contain at least one of: (a) a path/to/file.py::<symbol> reference (resolved by register_symbol_refs_check from bead D); (b) a docs/failure-modes.md row id (e.g., row-42 or a Failure-mode cell match); (c) a tests/test_<module>.py::<test_name> reference. Patterns without any citation fail.

Wire pattern_citation_check into the CLAUDE.md pre-commit chain in scripts/hooks/install.sh (after the existing claude_model_tags_check). Smoke test in tests/hooks/parsers.bats against the live CLAUDE.md is the migration deadline for any uncited legacy entries. Corrupted-pattern fixture asserts the hook fires.

Style invariant this depends on (already pinned in CLAUDE.md): ## for top-level sections, ### for pattern entries inside ## Discovered Patterns.

See docs/upstream-harness-improvements.md § 2026-04-29 (runaway-loop entry) Change 1.
acceptance_criteria: pattern_citation_check exists in parsers.sh; passes against current CLAUDE.md (retrofit any uncited entries in this same bead). install.sh wires it. tests/hooks/parsers.bats: accept (entry with path::symbol citation), accept (entry with failure-mode row id), accept (entry with tests/ reference), reject (entry with no citation), smoke against live CLAUDE.md returns 0 after retrofit. Lists every uncited entry on failure.
